$comment: Constant-time-shape error responses for 401 / 404 (Shape R, access-failure class). Per wire/error-responses.md, ALL access-failure outcomes (401 lease invalid / 404 service undeclared / 404 allowedSubs-mismatch collapsed from 403 per cloister-aa9376 / 429 rate-limited) collapse to byte-equal Shape R bodies. The route boundary owns the wire contract; vault DO emits structured shapes internally but the route rewraps. This corpus pins the X-2 unification from cloister-6eba0a (2026-05-18 cycle) — distinct 403 body is HISTORY, the new canonical Shape R applies. See ambiguity note resolution in conformance/README.md.

vectors.name: unauthorized_401_lease_invalid
vectors.description: 401 response when Interlace lease fails verification per interlace-spec/0.1.0/ §3.2. Body shape pinned. sha256 lets a second impl assert byte-equality without re-parsing the JSON.

vectors.name: not_found_404_allowed_subs_collapsed
vectors.description: 404 response when lease is valid but peerFp doesn't match the credential's allowedSubs glob — historically a 403 'forbidden' shape, now collapsed to a 404 Shape R per cloister-aa9376 + 2026-05-18 cycle X-2 (cloister-6eba0a). The spec MUST NOT distinguish 'credential exists, peer not allowed' from 'no credential for this service' from 'service undeclared' — all three return the same Shape R body. This is the per-peer + per-service enumeration-oracle closure.

vectors.name: not_found_404_service_undeclared
vectors.description: 404 when <service> is not a vaultProxyService in the manifest. Body MATCHES the 401 + allowed-subs-mismatch cases byte-for-byte — same constant-time Shape R so the caller cannot use this endpoint to enumerate which services the operator has declared.
